# exímIA Academy — Brownfield Architecture Assessment

**Generated:** 2026-02-15
**Generated By:** @architect (Aria)
**Mode:** YOLO (Autonomous)
**Template:** brownfield-architecture-template-v2

---

## 1. Introduction

This document captures the current architectural state of exímIA Academy — an AI-powered educational platform with Socratic dialogue, designed for universities and enterprises. It serves as the foundational reference for all future enhancements, ensuring new development integrates seamlessly with existing patterns.

### Change Log

| Change | Date | Version | Description | Author |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|--------|
| Initial Assessment | 2026-02-15 | 1.0 | Full brownfield architecture scan | @architect (Aria) |

---

## 2. Existing Project Analysis

### 2.1 Current Project State

- **Primary Purpose:** AI-powered Socratic learning platform (multi-tenant SaaS)
- **Current Tech Stack:** Next.js 15 + TypeScript 5 + Supabase + Vercel AI SDK + Tailwind CSS 4
- **Architecture Style:** Monorepo (Turborepo + pnpm) with shared packages, App Router, server-first
- **Deployment Method:** Vercel (web app) + Supabase Cloud (database/auth) + Upstash Redis (rate limiting)

### 2.2 Available Documentation

- `docs/prd.md` — Product Requirements Document
- `docs/architecture.md` — Original system architecture
- `docs/design-system-guide.md` — 29+ component catalog with props/usage
- `docs/shock-report.html` — Design token source of truth
- `docs/screens.md` — UI screen definitions
- `docs/epics/` — 15 epics (1-15) covering full roadmap
- `docs/qa/` — QA review reports per epic
- `docs/guides/` — SSO setup guides (Azure AD, Okta)

### 2.3 Identified Constraints

- Supabase RLS policies tightly coupled to `auth_tenant_id()` and `auth_user_role()` — all new tables MUST implement RLS
- `courses.tenant_id` and `courses.created_by` are NOT NULL with no auto-trigger — must be set explicitly
- `teacher` role removed — use `manager` everywhere
- `mode` column removed from schema — no dual-mode support
- AI SDK structured output uses native `output_format: { type: "json_schema" }` — NOT tool_use pattern
- Supabase `.upsert()` failures are SILENT when columns don't exist in schema cache

## 4. Workspace Architecture

### 4.1 Monorepo Packages

```
eximia-academy/                    # Root (pnpm workspace)
├── apps/
│   └── web/                       # @eximia/web — Next.js 15 App
├── packages/
│   ├── agents/                    # @eximia/agents — AI orchestration (8 agents)
│   ├── database/                  # @eximia/database — Drizzle ORM + schema
│   ├── shared/                    # @eximia/shared — Types, constants, validators
│   └── ui/                        # @eximia/ui — Design system (37 components)
├── supabase/                      # Migrations (27), seed, config
├── dialectica/                    # Python: content analysis
├── microservice/                  # Python: blueprint generation (FastAPI)
├── docs/                          # PRD, architecture, epics, guides
├── Squads/                        # AIOS squad configurations
├── Benchmarks/                    # AI benchmarking data
└── tests/                         # E2E tests (Playwright)
```

### 4.2 Package Dependency Graph

```
@eximia/web
├── @eximia/agents
│   ├── @eximia/shared
│   ├── @ai-sdk/anthropic
│   ├── ai (Vercel AI SDK)
│   └── zod
├── @eximia/database
│   ├── drizzle-orm
│   └── postgres
├── @eximia/shared
│   └── zod
└── @eximia/ui
    ├── react
    ├── class-variance-authority
    ├── clsx
    └── tailwind-merge
```

---

## 5. AI Agent Pipeline

### 5.1 Architecture

```
orchestrateSocraticDialogue(input)
    │
    ├── Socrates Agent  ─── generates dialogue response
    │       ↓
    ├── Editor Agent    ─── refines/edits response
    │       ↓
    └── Tester Agent    ─── validates quality (retry on fail)

organizeContent(rawText)
    │
    ├── Auto-chunking   ─── >60KB → split by paragraphs
    ├── processChunk    ─── generateObject per chunk (retry x2)
    └── mergeResults    ─── deduplicate + combine

Additional Agents:
    ├── Analyst         ─── AI detection analysis
    ├── Creator         ─── Content creation
    ├── Enricher        ─── Content enrichment
    └── Profiler        ─── Student profiling (Big Five, Enneagram, DISC)
```

### 5.2 Agent Configuration

- **Model:** `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`
- **Structured Output:** Native JSON schema (NOT tool_use)
- **Max Retries:** 2 attempts
- **Timeout:** 180 seconds default
- **Chunk Size:** 60,000 chars max, 500 char overlap
- **Retryable Errors:** 429, network, timeout, 500-502
- **Non-Retryable:** Validation/schema parsing errors

### 5.3 Each Agent Structure

```
packages/agents/src/
├── {agent}.ts              # Agent logic (generateObject call)
├── prompts/{agent}.ts      # System prompt template
└── schemas/{agent}.ts      # Zod output schema
```

---

## 6. Database Schema

### 6.1 Tables (18 schema modules via Drizzle ORM)

| Table | Purpose | RLS |
|-------|---------|-----|
| `users` | User profiles + auth metadata | Yes |
| `tenants` | Multi-tenant config (whitelabel) | Yes |
| `courses` | Course definitions | Yes |
| `chapters` | Chapter content (block editor) | Yes |
| `questions` | Quiz questions (AI-generated) | Yes |
| `sessions` | Socratic dialogue sessions | Yes |
| `messages` | Chat messages per session | Yes |
| `enrollments` | Student-course relationships | Yes |
| `areas` | Learning areas/categories | Yes |
| `user_areas` | User-area assignments | Yes |
| `analyses` | AI analysis results | Yes |
| `qa_reports` | Quality assurance reports | Yes |
| `content_ingestions` | Upload/import job tracking | Yes |
| `question_generation_jobs` | Question gen task tracking | Yes |
| `platform_audit_log` | Activity audit (super admin) | Yes |
| `materials` | Course materials/resources | Yes |
| `course_enrichment` | AI enrichment data | Yes |
| `blueprint_generation` | Blueprint generation jobs | Yes |

### 6.2 Migration History (27 migrations)

Key milestones:
- `20260207` — Initial schema + RLS foundation
- `20260208` — Socratic RPC, remove dual mode, soft delete, LGPD
- `20260209` — Super admin, whitelabel, onboarding
- `20260210` — Areas, role unification (teacher→manager), multimodal, block editor
- `20260211` — Content ingestion, question generation, PPTX support
- `20260212` — Course enrichment
- `20260213` — Blueprint generation, chapter ordering
- `20260214` — Biblioteca (books) schema
- `20260215` — Book processing status

### 6.3 Critical Schema Rules

- ALL tables use RLS with `auth_tenant_id()` + `auth_user_role()`
- Soft delete pattern: `is_deleted` column (LGPD compliance)
- JSONB for flexible profile data (student profiling)
- `tenant_id` required on all tenant-scoped tables
- No auto-triggers for `courses.tenant_id` / `courses.created_by` — must set explicitly

## 7. App Routes & API Surface

### 7.1 Route Groups

| Group | Routes | Auth Required | Layout |
|-------|--------|---------------|--------|
| `(auth)` | login, accept-invite, reset-password | No | Auth layout |
| `(platform)` | dashboard, courses, biblioteca, admin, analytics, perfil | Yes | Platform layout (sidebar) |
| `super-admin` | tenants, dashboard, audit | Yes (super_admin role) | Super admin layout |
| `onboarding` | onboarding flow | Yes | Minimal |
| `api/` | 46+ REST endpoints | Varies | — |

### 7.2 API Route Categories

| Category | Count | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| Auth | 1 | OAuth callback |
| Privacy | 2 | LGPD export/delete |
| Ingestion | 8 | Content upload pipeline |
| Courses | 3 | Question gen, enrichment |
| Chapters | 1 | Chapter question gen |
| Admin | 13 | Areas, users, books, SSO |
| Jobs | 3 | Generation/enrichment status |
| Blueprint | 3 | Blueprint generation |
| Analytics | 3 | Student/manager analytics |
| Sessions | 1 | Chat messages |
| Super Admin | 5 | Tenant management |
| Profile | 1 | AI profile generation |

## 8. Security Architecture

### 8.1 Authentication

- **Provider:** Supabase Auth (@supabase/ssr)
- **Methods:** Email/password, OAuth (callback handler), SSO (Azure AD, Okta)
- **Session:** Server-side via createServerClient
- **Middleware:** Auth enforcement on protected routes

### 8.2 Authorization

- **Roles:** `student`, `manager`, `super_admin`
- **RLS:** Row-Level Security on all tables via `auth_tenant_id()`, `auth_user_role()`
- **Super Admin:** Separate route group + cookie-based active tenant (`x-sa-active-tenant`)

### 8.3 Rate Limiting (Upstash Redis)

| Limiter | Rate | Scope |
|---------|------|-------|
| `authLimiter` | 5 req/min | Per IP |
| `catchAllLimiter` | 100 req/min | Per IP |
| `chatLimiter` | 10 req/min | Per user |
| `questionGenLimiter` | 5 req/5min | Per user |
| `courseCreateLimiter` | 20 req/hour | Per user |
| `privacyLimiter` | 3 req/min | Per user |

### 8.4 Multi-Tenant Isolation

- **Resolution:** Subdomain-based (prod) + query param (dev)
- **Data Isolation:** RLS policies scoped to `auth_tenant_id()`
- **Storage:** Tenant-scoped storage buckets
- **Whitelabel:** Per-tenant theming/branding

---

## 9. Testing Strategy

### 9.1 Current Test Infrastructure

| Layer | Framework | Location | Config |
|-------|-----------|----------|--------|
| Unit | Vitest 3.0 | `**/__tests__/` | `apps/web/vitest.config.ts` |
| Component | Testing Library + jsdom | `packages/ui/src/__tests__/` | — |
| E2E | Playwright | `tests/e2e/` | `playwright.config.ts` |
| E2E Mocking | MSW v2 | `tests/e2e/mocks/` | instrumentation.ts |
| Validation | Zod | Inline schemas | — |

### 9.2 E2E Test Specs

- `teacher-course-creation.spec.ts` — Course CRUD flow
- `student-journey.spec.ts` — Student learning flow
- `manager-dashboard.spec.ts` — Dashboard analytics

### 9.3 E2E Mocking Strategy

- MSW `setupServer` activated via `instrumentation.ts` with `E2E_TESTING=true`
- Agent detection uses unique identifiers (`Harven_Socrates`, `Harven_Editor`, etc.)
- Fixtures in `tests/e2e/fixtures/` for each agent response
- Fake `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` required (SDK validates key existence)

---

## 10. Infrastructure & Deployment

### 10.1 Current Setup

- **Hosting:** Vercel (Next.js)
- **Database:** Supabase Cloud (PostgreSQL)
- **Cache/Rate Limit:** Upstash Redis
- **Monitoring:** Sentry (errors), PostHog (analytics), Vercel (speed insights)
- **CI/CD:** GitHub Actions (2 workflows)

### 10.2 CI/CD Pipelines

**ci.yml** — On push/PR to main:
1. Install dependencies (pnpm, frozen lockfile)
2. Lint (Biome)
3. TypeScript typecheck
4. Run Vitest tests
5. Production build

**e2e.yml** — On push/PR to main:
1. Install + Playwright browsers
2. Start Supabase local instance
3. Run seed
4. Build Next.js
5. Run Playwright E2E tests
6. Upload artifacts on failure (7-day retention)

---

## 11. Coding Standards

### 11.1 Enforced Standards

- **Formatter:** Biome — 2 spaces, no semicolons, 100 char line width
- **Imports:** Organized imports enabled
- **Linting:** Recommended + a11y + custom rules
- **Types:** TypeScript strict mode, no implicit any
- **Components:** CVA + cn() + forwardRef pattern
- **Tokens:** Tailwind v4 `@theme` directive, CSS variables (`var(--color-*)`)
- **No hardcoded colors:** Use theme tokens (`bg-bg-card`, `text-text-primary`)

### 11.2 Naming Conventions

- **Files:** kebab-case (`course-enrichment.ts`)
- **Components:** PascalCase (`ChatMessage.tsx`)
- **Routes:** kebab-case directories (`generate-questions/`)
- **Database:** snake_case (Drizzle schema)
- **CSS tokens:** kebab-case (`--color-accent-blue-mid`)

### 11.3 Patterns to Follow

- Server Components by default, `"use client"` only when needed
- Server Actions in `lib/actions/` directory
- Supabase client variants: `createClient()` (browser), `createServerClient()` (RSC), `createServiceClient()` (admin)
- Zod schemas for all validation boundaries
- Error boundaries for graceful error handling

---

## 12. Component Architecture Summary

### 12.1 Design System (@eximia/ui)

```
packages/ui/src/
├── atoms/          # Button, Input, Label, Badge, Avatar, etc. (11)
├── molecules/      # Card, Tabs, FormField, Accordion, etc. (12)
├── organisms/      # Modal, Sidebar, DataTable, Toast, etc. (14)
├── primitives/     # Radix UI wrappers
├── lib/            # cn() utility
├── styles/         # Base styles
├── tokens/         # Design tokens
└── __tests__/      # 12 component tests
```

### 12.2 App Components (apps/web/src/components/)

```
components/         # 105 app-specific components
├── auth/           # Login, password flows
├── layout/         # Header, sidebar, footer
├── providers/      # Auth, query, toast context
├── admin/          # Admin panel views
├── course/         # Course CRUD components
├── chapter/        # Chapter management
├── session/        # Socratic session UI
├── book/           # Biblioteca feature
├── analytics/      # Dashboard charts
├── blueprint/      # Blueprint generation
├── materiais/      # Materials management
├── onboarding/     # Onboarding flow
├── profile/        # User profile
├── super-admin/    # Tenant management
└── error-boundary.tsx
```

## 14. Integration Rules for Future Development

### MUST follow:

1. **RLS on every table** — No exceptions. Use `auth_tenant_id()` + `auth_user_role()`
2. **Use @eximia/ui components** — Never create ad-hoc HTML/CSS for elements that exist in the library
3. **Tailwind theme tokens only** — Zero hardcoded hex/rgba values
4. **Zod validation at boundaries** — All API inputs, form data, agent outputs
5. **Biome compliance** — 2 spaces, no semicolons, 100 char width
6. **Server Components first** — `"use client"` only when necessary
7. **AI SDK structured output** — Use `generateObject` with Zod schemas, NOT raw completions
8. **Rate limiting** — All new public-facing API routes must have rate limiters
9. **Multi-tenant scoping** — All queries must filter by tenant
10. **Migration files** — Schema changes via Supabase migrations, NOT direct SQL

### MUST NOT:

1. Use `teacher` role (removed — use `manager`)
2. Use `mode` column (removed)
3. Push directly (use @devops)
4. Skip RLS policies on new tables
5. Use hardcoded colors/values instead of theme tokens
6. Create new components without checking @eximia/ui first
7. Use tool_use pattern for structured output (use native JSON schema)

---

## 15. Next Steps

### For Story Manager (@sm)

Reference this document when creating stories. Key constraints:
- All new features must integrate with existing multi-tenant RLS
- Use existing package structure (@eximia/agents, @eximia/ui, @eximia/shared, @eximia/database)
- Follow the agent pipeline pattern for any new AI features
- Ensure E2E test coverage for critical flows

### For Developer (@dev)

- Read `docs/design-system-guide.md` before any UI work
- Follow existing patterns in `apps/web/src/lib/` for utilities
- Use `createServerClient()` for server-side Supabase access
- Add Vitest tests alongside implementation
- Schema changes require migration files in `supabase/migrations/`
